frameworks.name: Three Levels of Salesmanship
frameworks.definition: A progression from solo selling (Level 1) to recruiting individual salespeople (Level 2) to building and leading a team of salespeople (Level 3); each level requires a distinct identity and skill set.

claims.text: Alex Hormozi asserts that for roofing company founders who do all their own sales, scaling requires a three-stage transition: Stage 1 is selling personally, Stage 2 is recruiting one other person to sell on your behalf, and Stage 3 is recruiting and leading a team of salespeople. Founders who describe themselves as great at selling are operating only at Stage 1.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: roofing company or comparable field-sales business
founder is currently the primary or sole salesperson

claims.text: Alex Hormozi argues that a founder transitioning from solo sales to leading a sales team must shift their identity from being a skilled closer to being a skilled sales trainer and sales leader. The measure of success is no longer how well the founder closes, but how well the founder can teach others to close on their behalf.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: founder has already proven personal sales ability
ready to scale beyond single-person sales capacity
claims.mechanism: A founder who remains attached to being the best closer cannot shift mental bandwidth toward coaching and system-building, which is the actual constraint on team-based scaling.

claims.text: Alex Hormozi contends that recruiting salespeople to a roofing company is not a structural or economic problem but a mental one for early-stage founders. The practical path is straightforward: offer a commission-based arrangement and teach the recruit a concrete acquisition method such as door knocking.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: founder is new to team building
claims.mechanism: Founders new to hiring overthink recruitment because they are early in the business; the barrier is psychological, not structural, because a commission-plus-training offer is economically sufficient for candidates willing to work in sales.

practices.text: To recruit a first salesperson for a roofing company, offer a stated commission rate, demonstrate the door-knocking acquisition method, and allow the recruit to earn on results. No complex compensation structure or formal HR process is required at early stage.
When transitioning from solo sales to team sales, stop measuring yourself by your personal close rate and start measuring yourself by your team's close rate.
